data.title: Terra founder Do Kwon gets 15-year prison sentence for $40B crypto fraud, described as 'epic' & 'generational.'
data.description: Do Kwon, a cryptocurrency fraudster, has received a 15-year prison sentence for his involvement in the collapse of the Terra (CRYPTO: LUNA) cryptocurrency, leading to a significant market crash. U.S. District Judge Paul A. Engelmayer, who issued the sentence, criticized Kwon for his continuous deception of investors who had entrusted their life savings to him. The judge labeled the fraud as "epic" and "generational," emphasizing the severe damage caused. This case is considered one of the most harmful frauds in the history of federal prosecution. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: China sets economic tone for 2026 at Central Economic Work Conference, focusing on strengthening domestic demand.
data.description: In a report published during the annual Central Economic Work Conference in Beijing, CGTN highlighted China's focus for 2026 on enhancing domestic demand and progressing towards economic goals. The conference saw Chinese leaders convene to strategize on key economic issues. The agenda reveals China's determination to bolster its domestic market, emphasizing self-sufficiency. This move signals a shift towards prioritizing internal consumption over reliance on foreign markets. The emphasis on domestic demand aligns with China's broader economic strategy, supporting sustainable growth.

data.title: Yum China increases share repurchase authorization by $1 billion to $5.4 billion.
data.description: In Shanghai on December 11, 2025, Yum China Holdings, Inc. revealed that its Board of Directors has decided to boost the Company's share repurchase authorization by $1 billion. This brings the total aggregate authorization to $5.4 billion. The Company trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol YUMC and on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol 9987.

data.title: BTC rises post third Fed rate cut, analysts foresee bigger rally in crypto market following typical post-cut trend.
data.description: Following the Federal Reserve's third interest rate cut this year, the cryptocurrency markets experienced a recovery. Market analysts anticipate a significant increase to occur in line with the usual post-cut trend.

data.title: Largest Korean startup festival, COMEUP 2025, starts Dec 10 in Seoul, spanning Dec 10-12 at COEX Seoul with conferences, exhibitions, IR pitching.
data.description: The biggest startup event in Korea, COMEUP 2025, will commence on December 10 (KST), uniting groundbreaking startups, global and local investors, and prominent corporations. Scheduled to run from December 10 to 12 at COEX Seoul, this three-day festival will showcase conferences, exhibitions, IR pitching sessions, and business networking opportunities.

data.title: Amazon Game Studios and Crystal Dynamics announce 2 new Tomb Raider games at The Game Awards: Tomb Raider: Catalyst and Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis.
data.description: In Seattle, Amazon Game Studios and Crystal Dynamics made a big splash at The Game Awards by unveiling two new entries in the popular Tomb Raider series. The event showcased a sneak peek of Tomb Raider: Catalyst, the eagerly awaited upcoming installment in Lara Croft's journey, currently in progress. Additionally, fans were caught off guard with the announcement of Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, a complete reimagining of Lara Croft's classic first quest. Both games generated excitement and anticipation among viewers.
